summary: Serialize genre values into the manifest — `genres` on the kind, genre-fence extraction (fenced + TOML), the leaf/keyed-collection schema with structural addresses

per.section: Genre values — prose that declares its own anatomy

new.description: Prove: a genre fence (info string `genre.decision surface-authority`, TOML interior with leaf fields as multi-line strings and sibling collections as keyed tables `[rejected.baked-projection]`) extracts to a genre value whose leaves are addressed structurally (member, genre key, field path) and whose siblings are keyed, never positional (`20-surface.md`, the two leaf Decisions); the value round-trips through the manifest byte-identically (idempotence).

edit.description: Add the optional `genres` vocabulary to the kind object (`15-kinds.md`, "genres (optional)"): typed shapes — named fields over prose leaves plus keyed collections — parsed from the KIND.md header, load-guarded. Compose the `Fenced` primitive (FENCED-PRIMITIVE) with a TOML parse of each matching fenced block into a genre value's features, keyed by the fence info string's genre+key. No predicates here — the shape is the kind's, any clause over it is the bound package's (out of fence).

Add the `GenreValue`/leaf types and a `Features.genres` field: a leaf is an authored string addressed by member + genre key + field path (structural, stable under content edits); sibling collections are keyed (`rejected.baked-projection.because`), never positional (`20-surface.md`, "leaf addresses are structural and keyed"). Leaves carry no mentions at the floor (interpolation stays altitude-only). The extractor folds parsed genre values into `Features` beside `sections`/`directives`.

Serialize `Features.genres` into the `[[member]]` manifest table in one shape and deserialize it back (the member round-trip; FENCED-PRIMITIVE already serializes raw `fenced_blocks` at compose.rs ~1842 — the typed genre layer sits beside it, round-trip test near the fenced-blocks case ~3108): genre values are serialized whole (`15-kinds.md`), leaf fields as strings, sibling collections as keyed sub-tables, format-preserving via `toml_edit`. Every consumer stays carriage-blind — a document-carried and a (future) module-carried genre value serialize identically.

tests.asserts: genre-fence extraction yields structurally-addressed leaves and keyed siblings; the manifest serialization round-trips a genre value byte-identically; an unfenced block is plain prose (no genre value, no error) — opt-in per block.

acceptance: A kind declaring `genres` extracts each genre fence into a genre value with structural leaf addresses and keyed sibling collections; the value serializes whole into the manifest and round-trips idempotently through `toml_edit`; an unfenced block stays plain prose; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green.
notes: The manifest leaf schema — the read family (IMPACT-LEAF-GRAIN, CONTEXT-VERB) consumes it. FENCED-PRIMITIVE shipped (raw `fenced_blocks` + primitive), so this now composes it into typed genre values. Genre-value *predicates* and the corpus's own genre declarations (packages/**, kinds/**) are OUT of fence — the interactive session lands them after this ships.

summary: Give `impact` leaf grain — accept a leaf address and report citations separately from fallout, reading the manifest's serialized genre values

per.section: Decision: the CLI gains a read family — `why` and `requirements`

edit.description: Extend `impact` to accept a **leaf address** (member, genre key, field path) as well as a member name (`20-surface.md`, "The family gains leaf grain"): when the target resolves to a leaf, report **citations separately from fallout** — a declared one-way edge (a mention/citation) targeting the leaf is resolution-checked and reported as a citation, obligation-free; deleting or rewording a cited leaf is never blocked (`45-governance.md`, address grain). Reads only the manifest's serialized genre values — offline, tier-1, no runtime.

Widen the `Impact` clap arg to a member-or-leaf address and thread the manifest's serialized genre values (GENRE-MANIFEST-LEAF) into the `read::impact` call beside the existing feature/roster/edge inputs (~line 626), so a leaf address dispatches to leaf-grain reporting.

tests.asserts: `impact` on a leaf address reports the leaf's citations separately from its fallout; a citation to a leaf resolves against the manifest and never gates; a member-name target behaves exactly as before (regression).

acceptance: `temper impact <leaf-address>` reports at leaf grain — citations distinct from fallout — reading only the manifest's serialized genre values; the member-name path is unchanged; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green.
notes: blockedBy GENRE-MANIFEST-LEAF (needs the serialized leaf shape to read). Serialized before CONTEXT-VERB — both edit read.rs + main.rs, so they cannot run parallel. Citation reporting rides declared one-way edges (obligation-free, `45-governance.md`); no new obligating semantics.

summary: Add `temper context <address>` — emit a member's or leaf's declared neighborhood, with the mixed-rung coverage disclosure shipped in the same entry

per.section: Decision: the CLI gains a read family — `why` and `requirements`

edit.description: Add a `context` read function: given a member or leaf address, emit its declared neighborhood — genre slot, siblings, citers, satisfied requirements (`20-surface.md`, "`context <address>` emits a member's or leaf's declared neighborhood"), the pre-edit context bundle for the primary author. Consumes only the manifest (offline, tier-1). MUST **disclose coverage** in the same entry: every leaf-grain answer names what it cannot see ("N documents below rung 3 — not represented at leaf grain"), since a mixed-rung corpus is the standing state (`00-intent.md` genre Decision; law 1 — an incomplete answer in complete clothes erodes the verb).

Add a `Context { address }` clap subcommand dispatching to `read::context`, mapping the result to a projection (zero exit, no gate), beside `Why`/`Requirements`/`Impact` in the read family. Thread the manifest's serialized genre values so the neighborhood + coverage disclosure read from data `check` already computes.

tests.asserts: `context` on a leaf emits its genre slot, siblings, citers, and satisfied requirements from the manifest; the output discloses coverage (names the count of documents below rung 3 not represented at leaf grain); `context` never returns a non-zero exit.

acceptance: `temper context <address>` emits the declared neighborhood of a member or leaf from the manifest alone, and every leaf-grain answer discloses its mixed-rung coverage — the disclosure ships WITH the verb, never after; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green.
notes: The coverage disclosure is not a follow-on entry — the spec binds it into this one (`20-surface.md`: "the disclosure ships with the verbs, never after"). blockedBy IMPACT-LEAF-GRAIN (shares read.rs + main.rs — not parallel-safe). Last link of the addressable-corpus floor engine chain.

summary: Make `Primitive::Field` honor the nested key-path its doc-comment promises — held deferred until a nested-key kind (a JSON-manifest kind) consumes it

per.section: The extraction algebra — the soundness boundary, as data

gate.reason: no consumer — the Fenced half graduated to FENCED-PRIMITIVE (its genre-fence consumer arrived); the remaining key-path half has none. `Primitive::Field` reads a flat `fields.get(key)`, so a nested `a.b.c` path can't be read — but no current kind (skill/rule/memory) declares a nested key-path field. Revive when a kind demands it (a JSON-manifest kind's nested key-path field). NB `at-import` is the DIRECTIVES wave's edge primitive, not this.

edit.description: Make `Primitive::Field` honor the key-path its own doc-comment promises: the impl does a flat `unit.frontmatter.get(key)` in `apply`, so a nested `a.b.c` path can't be read. No new vocabulary member — this fixes the existing `field` primitive.

Key-path traversal for the field extractor: walk nested TOML/JSON tables to the leaf before `json_to_feature`, kind-preserving per the `type` lattice (`specs/architecture/10-contracts.md`). Absent (not errored) when the path doesn't resolve.

tests.asserts: a key-path `field` reads a nested value and is absent (not errored) when the path doesn't resolve; it preserves the source scalar kind.

acceptance: A nested-key-path `Primitive::Field` reads `a.b.c` over a `Unit` and is absent when unresolved; a KIND.md declaring it reads the intended feature; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test green. (Deferred: not built until a kind consumes it.)
notes: Narrowed 07-03: the Fenced half graduated to the open FENCED-PRIMITIVE (consumer arrived). Only the key-path `field` half remains, still no consumer. Shares kind.rs/extract.rs with the genre chain — serialize if un-deferred while that chain is live.

summary: Add the `agent` built-in harness kind — `.claude/agents/*.md` as a member document, read through the generic loader and checked by the embedded `agent.anthropic` package

per.section: Decision: package binding is by artifact kind

gate.reason: more built-in kinds is the wrong direction under the requirements/satisfies reframe; and post-adapter-wave the agent kind is mostly curated data (a KIND.md + PACKAGE.md, both outside build's fence) with near-zero engine code — revive only if a story demands the agent kind

new.description: The embedded built-in `agent` KIND.md (curated — humans author it; outside build's fence), under its provider beside kinds/claude-code/skill and kinds/claude-code/rule with a `provider = "claude-code"` line: `governs = { root = '.claude/agents', glob = '*.md' }`, the declared `yaml-frontmatter` projection format + unit shape, composed extraction over the `name`/`description`/optional `tools`/`model` fields plus markdown structure. build.rs auto-embeds it — no engine extractor.

The agent kind's built-in package `agent.anthropic` (curated product territory, outside build's fence), embedded with the std-lib by build.rs. Clauses: `name` required + `allowed_chars` `[a-z0-9-]`; `description` required; `forbidden_keys` for the Cursor `.mdc` keys Claude Code ignores; an advisory `max_lines`. Per-clause `source` citations to the Claude Code subagent docs.

Fixture-driven proof mirroring tests/rule_contract.rs: one broken agent per clause proving it fires, a clean agent that trips nothing, and an import round-trip insta snapshot proving idempotence — all through the generic adapter + loader, no per-kind engine code.

edit.description: Add `"agent"` to the `BUILTIN_KINDS` name-list so a `[kind.agent]` binding reads as a built-in layer, not a custom kind.
Add the `AGENT_PACKAGE = "agent.anthropic"` const beside SKILL_PACKAGE/RULE_PACKAGE and register its kind->package binding. build.rs auto-embeds the PACKAGE.md; no manual embed edit.

tests.asserts: each decidable clause of the agent package fires on its broken fixture and stays silent on a clean agent; import of an unchanged agent is idempotent (insta snapshot).

acceptance: `temper check` dispatches `.claude/agents/*.md` to the `agent.anthropic` package (conformance) and that package passes admissibility; `temper import` projects agents as one-document members idempotently via the generic frontmatter adapter; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Declared-adapter architecture: no src/agent.rs IR — the generic adapter projects it. Curated kinds/claude-code/agent/KIND.md + packages/agent.anthropic/PACKAGE.md sit outside build's fence; engine wiring is near-zero. Stays deferred: wrong direction under requirements/satisfies. Shares src/kind.rs with the genre chain + EXTRACTION-VOCAB-GAPS — serialize if un-deferred while either is live.

summary: Ship acquisition channels — npm optionalDependencies binary wrapper, Homebrew, cargo install — auto-detected, fail-loud on a missing platform binary

per.section: Decision: acquisition rides the ecosystem's package managers

gate.reason: needs human release setup (npm org, Homebrew tap, signing/publish creds) before the mechanical wrappers can be validated end-to-end

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages, GitHub release assets, and the Homebrew tap; channel auto-detected. Distinct from the existing `.github/workflows/temper.yml` CI job.

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`, confirmed on disk). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages (Biome's pattern) plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, `specs/architecture/50-distribution.md`). Reconcile with the flume manifest (a non-private publish entry, or a split package dir) rather than clobbering the harness manifest. Scoped/prefixed names per the resolved (project-name) fork.

acceptance: A stranger can acquire the prebuilt binary via npm (platform-specific optionalDependencies), Homebrew, standalone release assets, or `cargo install`; a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip.
notes: Release infra, mostly outside `src/`; the cargo gates can't validate npm/Homebrew publish, and channels need human creds (npm org, tap, signing). NB: root `package.json` already exists as the *private* flume manifest — reconcile or split, never blindly create a second. Only `.github/workflows/temper.yml` (CI) exists — no release.yml yet. Parked until a human sets creds up.
